steps.title: Identify the progression
steps.content: ”When alive” marks the first stage where phytoplankton absorb carbon. The blank then leads to “after they die,” introducing the second stage where they sink and store carbon. The passage describes how this process unfolds chronologically.

steps.title: Match the transition
steps.content: ”Then” signals temporal sequencing—what naturally comes next in a process. The other choices either add detail (Specifically), signal opposition (By contrast), or express reluctant concession (Nevertheless)—none show temporal progression. That matches choice D — D is correct.
